title: Adding Spell Check Feature
excerpt: Having the spell check feature for grammer and spellilng issue would help clean up any issues and present clear information, data when exported, etc.

posts.author: nfetsko (Natalie Fetsko)
posts.cookedHtml: Having the spell check feature for grammer and spellilng issue would help clean up any issues and present clear information, data when exported, etc.

posts.author: MelissaFox (Melissa Fox )
posts.cookedHtml:
Anyone else want spell check in the desktop version?

Having to copy and paste from other software to spell check items gets tiring. The desktop runs smoother on our systems - so having the browser open to use grammar plugins isn’t ideal. Fast fingers yield lots of typos!

posts.author: carrie.davis (Carrie)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, I just realized that my notes were not going through a spell check! UGGG, I’m usually moving really fast trying to capture thoughts during meetings, etc. and I need this tool… so I don’t look like a tool!

posts.author: anders_atlant3d (anders)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is such a miss from the monday team
Spell check is already baked into OSX they just need to activate the native spellchecker.

Apple Developer Documentation

NSSpellChecker | Apple Developer Documentation

An interface to the Cocoa spell-checking service.

please monday team fix this

posts.author: norman.hembc (Norman)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s been a couple of years since the first post on this topic, any progress on spellcheck in Monday.com?

posts.author: TBeaumont (T)
posts.cookedHtml: This was a problem I had with Monday. The best way to fix it that I have found is to turn on the built in spell checker for my browser. (I use Chrome).
